task_recall.notes: The agent identifies all tasks including the Sharma Bakery completed order, the three active orders (Eastern Command, SATA, Tezpur), and critically the implicit proactive outreach task for Sharma Bakery (Subtask 5 under Parent Task 1). The year-end financial tasks are a bonus. No meaningful task omissions detected.

next_step_quality.notes: The proactive outreach next step is well-specified: Ashish personally sends a message to Sharma Bakery, acknowledges completed order, confirms satisfaction, probes for upcoming requirements. The agent also correctly notes not to delegate this to staff — a relationship-maintenance nuance. Next steps across all tasks are concrete and assignable. Minor over-engineering with 13 subtasks for Eastern Command, but the quality of individual next steps is high.

implicit_task_detection.notes: The agent correctly detects the ~4 week silence gap (26 Feb closed, 26 Mar current date) and surfaces a proactive reorder outreach task as Subtask 5 under Sharma Bakery — exactly matching the expected output. The detection is explicit: 'No new enquiry or message visible from Sharma Bakery in any thread. At this silence duration for a bakery client, a proactive check-in is warranted.' Additionally detects: transit damage inspection for inter-state goods, gate pass requirements for Army deliveries, year-end financial tasks, and the non-response gap on Tezpur. All high-value implicit detections.

passes: Surfaces the proactive Sharma Bakery check-in task (Subtask 5) with explicit reference to ~4 week silence since 26 Feb order close
Correctly frames the outreach as relationship maintenance and assigns it to Ashish personally rather than staff
Next step for Sharma Bakery outreach covers all expected elements: acknowledge completed order, confirm satisfaction, probe for upcoming requirements
Correctly identifies current date as 26 Mar from All Staff group activity and uses this to calculate the gap
Does not treat order closure as a terminal state — forward-looking tasks are created
Cross-thread correlation of Sharma Bakery order (Thread 1 + Thread 2 Feb cluster) is clean and accurate
Tezpur zero-information gap is correctly flagged as a blind spot with escalation action
SATA inter-state goods identified as requiring e-way bill and transit damage inspection — operationally sound
failures: The proactive outreach task is placed as Subtask 5 under a parent task rather than surfaced as a primary implicit task — slightly buries the signal though it is present and clearly articulated

Subtask 4 (post-delivery feedback) partially overlaps with Subtask 5 (proactive reorder outreach) without distinguishing that after 4 weeks, feedback and reorder outreach collapse into a single action — minor structural redundancy
Year-end financial tasks (Parent Task 5) are calendar-triggered assumptions not grounded in thread evidence — adds noise, though not harmful to core pass criteria
notes: The agent clearly passes the core criterion: it detects the 4-week silence on Sharma Bakery and creates an explicit, well-reasoned proactive outreach task assigned to Ashish with correct framing. The output is substantially more detailed than the expected minimum, which is operationally useful but introduces some redundancy. The Sharma Bakery outreach task (Subtask 5) is the exact behaviour the test requires and it is present, specific, and actionable. The agent also demonstrates strong implicit task detection across the other active orders. Overall a high-quality response with minor structural inefficiencies.
